name: Independent Code Review
description: Changes are reviewed by an independent code reviewer prior to code being merged and released to production. Independent code review restricts the ability for anyone individual from taking a change production without involving an independent party (4-eyes principle).

attributes.text: Code Reviews are performed prior to committing a change to the main branch
testable_criteria: A code review record (e.g. pull request) exists for the change.
The code review was completed (approved) before the change was merged to the main branch.
The merge commit timestamp to main is on or after the code review approval timestamp.
No changes were committed directly to the main branch bypassing the code review process.

attributes.text: Code Review approvals are performed by independent code reviewers
testable_criteria: The approving reviewer is a different individual than the author of the change.
The approving reviewer did not commit or co-author any of the code changes in the pull request.
At least one approval from an independent reviewer was recorded prior to merge.

attributes.text: Testing is performed in accordance with the testing policy
testable_criteria: Unit tests for new code achieve minimum 80% line coverage.
Branch coverage is ≥ 70%.
Function coverage is ≥ 80%.
Integration tests achieve minimum 60% coverage for critical integration points.
IF the change touches security-sensitive, payment, or authentication flows THEN coverage for those paths = 100%.
IF the change modifies legacy code THEN coverage is maintained or improved relative to prior levels.
A coverage report was generated and reviewed as part of the code review.
All unit tests passed with zero failures prior to merge/release.
All integration tests passed prior to merge/release.
End-to-end tests covering critical user journeys passed prior to release.
IF applicable, performance tests met defined benchmarks.
Security test/scan results were reviewed and approved prior to release.
Tests were executed in the CI/CD pipeline before merge.
Test results are visible as pull request status checks.
No flaky tests were left unresolved (fixed or removed) before release.
No known critical or high-severity bugs are outstanding at release.
Manual smoke tests were completed for critical features prior to release.

IF the change qualifies for a documented exception (legacy integration, third-party config, PoC, documentation-only, build/deployment scripts, dependency version bump, or behavior-preserving refactor) THEN the exception is explicitly identified and justified.
